Japanese: ‥‥罪門さん。 なんとなく、わかる気がします。
English: Officer Marshall. I think I understand.

Japanese: あなたが《ＳＬ９号事件》に こだわる理由が。
English: I think I know why you care so much about the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: きっと‥‥サボテンの精の イタズラだろうさ。
English: Sounds like you've been sipping too much cactus juice, pardner.

Japanese: ここに‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》の 資料があります。
English: I have the SL-9 Incident file here.

Japanese: この中に、“罪門”の名前を 見つけました。
English: The name "Marshall" is mentioned in here...

Japanese: 連続殺人事件の、 被害者のヒトリです。
English: in a list of murder victims.

Japanese: 《罪門　直斗》‥‥ あなたと関係のある人物ですね？
English: "Neil Marshall"... Are you related to this man?

Japanese: “ざいもん　なおと”‥‥
English: Neil Marshall...?

Japanese: ‥‥ボウヤは当然、 聞いたことがあるだろうさ。
English: Yeah, I'm sure you've heard the name.

Japanese: なんせ、２年前‥‥
English: Two years ago...

Japanese: アンタと同じ、くだらねえ賞を もらった、名検事さんだからな。
English: he received the same lousy prosecutor award you got.

Japanese: なんですと！　検事‥‥
English: What!? A prosecutor...?

Japanese: （“賞”って‥‥やっぱり、 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》か）
English: (He must be talking about the King of Prosecutors award.)

Japanese: 思い出した‥‥罪門　直斗検事。
English: Now I remember... Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: 私が担当した《ＳＬ９号事件》の 前任者だった。
English: He handled the SL-9 case before I did.

Japanese: そうだ。アイツが殺されて‥‥ アンタが担当することになった。
English: That's right. He was killed...
and the case fell into your hands.
But what's his relation to you...?

Japanese: 罪門　直斗は‥‥オレの弟だ。
English: He was my brother.

Japanese: 当時の主席捜査官・巌徒　海慈と 組んで、捜査にあたっていた。
English: He was investigating the murders with Damon Gant, Chief Detective at the time.

Japanese: その下で動いていたのが、 オレたち捜査班だった。
English: The group of detectives I was part of worked under them.

Japanese: あの連続殺人犯を起訴するために、 みんな必死でね‥‥
English: We were desperate to prosecute the killer.

Japanese: 青影　丈‥‥‥
English: Joe Darke...

Japanese: 弟はヤツと格闘して、殺害された。 そして、その際‥‥
English: My brother fought Darke and was killed.

Japanese: 青影は初めて“証拠”を残した。 それがヤツの運のつき‥‥
English: That was the first time Darke left behind any evidence. That was all we needed.

Japanese: ついに起訴されて、判決を受けた。 ‥‥めでたし、めでたし。
English: He was arraigned and incarcerated. The case was finally closed...

Japanese: 公式記録には、そう書かれている。
English: at least, according to the public records.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、モンダイが？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 弟は、青影に殺されるような ヤツじゃない。
English: My brother couldn't have been killed by Joe Darke.

Japanese: 格闘の際の、ヤツの集中力‥‥ オレが、一番よく知っているさ。
English: I knew my brother better than anyone...
No one could have beaten him in a fight.

Japanese: ‥‥たったそれだけのコトで、 今回のようなバカなコトを‥‥？
English: And that's it? That's your reason for your insane actions?

Japanese: 弟がイノチを落としたウラには、 ゼッタイに“何か”ある。
English: There's more to my brother's death than what the records say.

Japanese: アンタたちが、隠そうとしても！ ‥‥オレは知っているのさ。
English: No matter how much you try to hide it, you can't fool me.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ これで、ハッキリしたようです。
English: Well... at least one thing's for certain.

Japanese: ‥‥事件当日。 警察局で、何があったか‥‥
English: Now we know what happened at the Police Department on the day of the crime.

Japanese: あの日は、《ＳＬ９号事件》の 再捜査権が失われる日だった。
English: That was the last day the SL-9 case could be reopened.

Japanese: ２年前の解決にナットクできない 罪門　恭介は、証拠の強奪を計画。
English: Not satisfied with its resolution, Officer Marshall planned to steal the evidence.

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官のＩＤを盗みだし、 彼になりすまして、証拠保管室へ。
English: Disguising himself as Detective Goodman, he entered the evidence room.

Japanese: そこで、原灰巡査に見とがめられた ため、キゼツさせて逃亡‥‥
English: Officer Meekins confronted him, so he rendered him unconscious and fled.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、これで ハッキリしたようだ。
English: Yes, this mystery has finally been cleared up.

Japanese: やはり、あの日。 警察局では、殺人事件はなかった！
English: No murder took place at the Police Department that day!

Japanese: グーゼンとは恐ろしい‥‥
English: The things that happen by chance never cease to amaze...

Japanese: 検事局で殺人があったのと、 まったく同じ時間‥‥
English: At exactly the same time as the murder at the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: 警察局にて“ニセモノ”の 格闘があったとは‥‥な。
English: this fake murder was going on at the Police Department.

Japanese: （“グーゼン”‥‥？ そんな、ツゴウのいい話が‥‥）
English: (Chance...? It's got to be more than just that...)

Japanese: 事件当日、警察局で殺人事件が なかった‥‥となれば。
English: So if no one was murdered at the Police Department on the day of the crime,

Japanese: 当然。検事局の地下駐車場の 事件こそ“事実”というコトです。
English: that means the murder in the Prosecutor's Office's parking lot was the real one.

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥やはり。 犯行が可能なのは１人きり、だ。
English: Which, in turn, means... only one person could have committed the crime:

Japanese: ‥‥宝月　巴主席検事。
English: Chief Prosecutor Lana Skye.

Japanese: し、しかし！　きのうの審理で、 結論は出なかったはず‥‥
English: B-but wait! A verdict wasn't reached in yesterday's trial!

Japanese: だから、今日！　こうして 警察局の事件を審理したのだ。
English: Which is why we examined the incident at the Police Department today.
But...!

Japanese: きのう、宝月　巴に判決が 下されなかった理由は、１つ。
English: There's only one reason the defendant was not convicted yesterday:

Japanese: 警察局で発生した“同時殺人”の ナゾが残されていたからだ。
English: there yet remained the mystery of the simultaneous murder at the Police Department.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ボウヤのほうが １枚、上手だったようだぜ‥‥
English: It seems to me... this boy's got the draw on you, pardner.

Japanese: 警察局の、すべてのナゾは解けた。 ムジュンするものは、もうない。
English: All the mysteries at the Police Department have been uncovered. No contradictions

Japanese: “殺人”は、検事局で発生した！ 可能だったのは、宝月　巴のみ。
English: remain. The murder took place at the Prosecutor's Office! The only suspect is Lana Skye.

Japanese: 目撃者・市ノ谷　響華の証言に、 マチガイはなかったのだ！
English: There were no errors in the testimony of the witness, Angel Starr!

Japanese: 何か反論があれば‥‥ ２．５文字以内でおねがいしよう。
English: If you have a response... make it one word or less.

Japanese: 《ぐッ！》
English: ARRRGHHHH!!!

Japanese: 以上、だ。
English: I rest my case.

Japanese: どうやら、結論が出たようです。 ギモンの余地は、ありません。
English: It seems this trial has reached its conclusion. There's no room for doubt.

Japanese: 警察局の《同時》殺人事件‥‥ 私が出るまでもなかったようだ。
English: Well done, Mr. Wright. Thanks to you, I didn't need to waste my time...

Japanese: ご協力をカンシャしよう。 ‥‥弁護人。
English: disproving the alleged "murder" at the Police Department.

Japanese: （‥‥今日、ぼくが立証したことに マチガイはなかったハズだ‥‥）
English: (There's no doubt what I proved today is true...)

Japanese: （防犯カメラに残された映像は、 ニセモノだった！）
English: (The apparent murder on the security camera's tape really was fake!)

Japanese: （でも、それが‥‥）
English: (But I didn't realize...)

Japanese: （ともえさんの有罪を 立証してしまうなんて‥‥！）
English: (that would end up proving Lana guilty!)

Japanese: それでは！　宝月　巴に 判決を言いわたします！
English: Now then, the time for the verdict has arrived. This court finds the defendant...

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと 待ってください！
English: Y-your Honor! Wait!

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃん！
English: Ema!

Japanese: ベンゴ側には‥‥その。 カガク的に、イギがありますっ！
English: The defense has an objection. A scientific objection!
Right?

Japanese: “ね”と言われても‥‥
English: What do you mean, "right?"

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたですか？ この子のホゴシャは。
English: Are you this girl's guardian?

Japanese: ‥‥え！ は、はあ‥‥まあ、なんと
English: Your Honor! Oh, uh, in a sense...

Japanese: おねがいします、５分だけ！ ‥‥あたしの話、聞いてください！
English: Please, Your Honor. All I'm asking is for a minute of your time!
Please hear me out!

Japanese: 御剣検事さん！　おねがい‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth, please...

Japanese: ギモンを残すのは、 私の望むところではない。
English: I don't want to leave any loose ends.

Japanese: ‥‥３分、時間を与えよう。
English: You want a minute? I'll give you three.

Japanese: あたし‥‥ショックだったんです。 《ＳＬ９号事件》が、まさか‥‥
English: I... I was kind of in shock.

Japanese: あの《青影事件》だった、なんて。
English: I mean, finding out the SL-9 incident referred to the Joe Darke Killings!

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Now that she mentions it...)

Japanese: （資料の中に、宝月姉妹の名前も 出てきていたっけ）
English: (the names of both Skye sisters were in that file.)

Japanese: でも。それで‥‥あたし。 なんとなく、わかったんです。
English: But that's when I figured it out.

Japanese: 罪門さんが、あの日。 何をやろうとしていたのか‥‥
English: I mean, what Officer Marshall was trying to do that day...

Japanese: だから、罪門さんの指紋は 事件にカンケイない、って。
English: So I knew his fingerprint had nothing to do with the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥残るのは、コレ。 もう１つの“手のアト”ですよね。
English: That left only one thing... the other handprint!

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事の保管庫から 発見された血痕、か‥‥
English: You mean the traces of blood found on Detective Gumshoe's locker...

Japanese: しかし‥‥その手形からは、 指紋は検出されなかったのでは？
English: But no fingerprints were found on it, right?

Japanese: でも！　カガク的に 調べれば、手がかりがあるかも！
English: No, but I figured if I examined it scientifically... I'd be sure to find a clue!

Japanese: そう思って‥‥ひとっ走り、 調べなおしてきたんです！
English: So I ran over there and looked at it again!

Japanese: ‥‥！ そ、それで！　何かわかったの？
English: So did you find something?

Japanese: ええと‥‥‥いいえっ！
English: Um... No!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ザンネンですが。 あたしのチカラでは、どうにも。
English: Sorry. I guess I'm not much of a scientific investigator after all.

Japanese: あの。それでおしまい‥‥？
English: Um... Is that all?

Japanese: なんといっても、あたし。 かよわい女子高生ですから。
English: Please don't be mad. I'm just a high school student!

Japanese: （こっちだって、 かよわい弁護士なんだけど）
English: (And I'm just an attorney...)

Japanese: でも、でも！　もう、手がかりは この血のアトしかないんです！
English: But Mr. Wright! Those traces of blood are the only clue we have!

Japanese: ここに、何か“ムジュン”を 見つけないと‥‥
English: If we can't find something wrong with them...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、プロだし。 あとは、おねがいしますっ！
English: Please, Mr. Wright. You're a professional.
If anyone can save Lana, it's you!

Japanese: え‥‥‥ ええええええええッ！
English: Me? Oh boy...

Japanese: ‥‥時間です。 コタエをうかがいましょう。
English: Time's up. Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 警察局の“事件”について‥‥
English: With regard to the incident at the Police Department...

Japanese: 何か、まだ“ギモン”が 残されているのですか？
English: Does any reasonable doubt remain?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護側は、 もう１つの血痕が気になるようだ。
English: It appears the defense is troubled by the other blood mark.

Japanese: 上面図で言えば‥‥このあたりに 付着していた“手のアト”だな。
English: Looking at the floor plans, a handprint was discovered around here.

Japanese: これに、何かモンダイでも？ ‥‥おじょうさん？
English: Is there a problem with this?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、ごめんなさい！ あたし、チカラになれなくて。
English: Mr. Wright! I'm sorry I can't be of more use...

Japanese: でも‥‥！
English: But still!

Japanese: この血痕に、何か“ムジュン”が ないと‥‥お姉ちゃんは！
English: If you can't find anything wrong with that blood mark, Lana will be...

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人！ これ以上は、待てませんぞ。
English: Please answer my question, Mr. Wright. We don't have all day.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ （よく、考えるんだ‥‥今こそ！）
English: Y-yes, Your Honor. (If ever I've needed to concentrate, it's now!)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事の保管庫に 残された、“手のアト”‥‥）
English: (What could be wrong with that handprint on Detective Gumshoe's locker?)

Japanese: （何か‥‥何か、ないのか！ “ムジュン”は‥‥）
English: (Could there be something I'm missing...?)

Japanese: （正直なトコロ‥‥ まだ、ハッキリしない！）
English: (I've got to be honest... I don't see anything wrong with it!)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
English: (Still...)

Japanese: （ここでぼくが立ち止まれば、 今度こそ、ともえさんは‥‥！）
English: (If I give up now, Lana's going to be convicted for sure...)

Japanese: 現場に残された、 この“手のアト”は‥‥
English: This handprint left at the crime scene...

Japanese: おそらく、アキラカに ムジュンしています！
English: clearly shows a contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥アキラカに目が 泳いでいるようだな‥‥弁護士。
English: The only thing that seems clear is you're grasping, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: さきほどから‥‥現場の上面図を 穴のあくほど見つめていますが‥‥
English: You've been staring pretty intently at those floor plans.

Japanese: そこに、何かモンダイが あるのですか？
English: Tell me... is there a problem with them?

Japanese: （やはり、オカシイ‥‥！）
English: (Yes, this is strange...!)

Japanese: よく見れば、この上面図‥‥ 何か“足りないモノ”があります。
English: Take a good look at these floor plans.
Something is missing.

Japanese: “足りないモノ”‥‥？ 描かれていない、というコトかな？
English: "Missing"...? You mean, something hasn't been drawn on there?

Japanese: そして、その“足りないモノ”を 描きこむことによって‥‥
English: Yes. Something that, when drawn,

Japanese: この血痕の“イミ”は、まったく ちがったモノになるのです！
English: will completely change the meaning of the blood mark!

Japanese: ‥‥今のコトバが、弁護人の 言い逃れでないことを祈ります。
English: Let us pray the defense isn't simply trying to buy time.

Japanese: それでは。うかがいましょう！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （今回は、やたら証拠品が たくさんある‥‥）
English: (With all this evidence here...)

Japanese: （何か、あるだろう‥‥）
English: (there's got to be something I can use!)

Japanese: 上面図に“足りないモノ”‥‥ いったい、なんですかな？
English: The question is... which item can prove something is missing in the floor plans?

Japanese: “ヘタなテッポウも、数を撃てば 当たる”‥‥西部のコトワザさ。
English: As they say back west... "Even a blind man can hit with buckshot."

Japanese: ただし‥‥弾丸がシメって いなければ、のハナシだがね。
English: That is... so long as he's facin' the general direction.

Japanese: どうやら。弁護士の弾丸は ビショビショのようだ‥‥
English: It seems Mr. Wright's not sure which direction to face.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　やたら証拠品が あるということは‥‥）
English: (It's no use!)

Japanese: （それだけ、ハズレが多いと いうことじゃないか‥‥！）
English: (The more evidence there is, the more wrong choices!)

Japanese: 事件があったときの、保管室‥‥ 落ちついて、思い出しましょう！
English: Just calm down, Mr. Wright.
Try and remember what the evidence room was like on the day of the crime!

Japanese: （なぜ‥‥この血痕が、妙に 気になるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What is it that bothers me about this blood mark..?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、おねがいします。 今度こそ、まちがいありません！
English: Please allow me another chance, Your Honor. I'll prove it this time!

Japanese: そのベニヤ板が、どうかしたかい。
English: What about that piece of plywood?

Japanese: 《タイホくん》‥‥ さすが、将来のマスコットです。
English: The Blue Badger! Mascot of the police force!

Japanese: 彼はその身をもって、 決定的な証拠を守っていました。
English: Defender of truth, guardian of proof!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ、弁護人。
English: Explain yourself, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 現場の上面図を見てください。 ここには‥‥“彼”がいません。
English: Please look at the floor plans of the crime scene. The Blue Badger is not here.

Japanese: ウスっぺらいヤツには、 ウスっぺらい友が、よく似合うさ。
English: So?

Japanese: それでは‥‥ウスっぺらい ぼくの友だちを置いてみましょう。
English: So watch what happens when we put him in.

Japanese: 事件当時、彼が おどっていた場所は‥‥ここです。
English: This is where he was dancing at the time of the crime.

Japanese: いかがですか？　“あるコト”に 気づきませんか‥‥？
English: Well...?

Japanese: “あるコト”と言われましても‥‥
English: Well... what?

Japanese: あ‥‥あああああ‥‥ッ！
English: ! *gasp!*

Japanese: そう。この場所でタイホくんが おどっているかぎり‥‥
English: That's right. So long as the Blue Badger is dancing here...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の保管庫に、 手のアトをつけるコトはできない！
English: it would be impossible... to place a handprint at this spot on the locker!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだとおおおッ‥‥！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥つまり！ どういうことなのですかッ！
English: So that means... Uh... just exactly what does that mean!?

Japanese: いや、だから！ 不可能なんですよ！
English: It means it can't be done!

Japanese: いやいや、だから！ ジッサイ、血痕は残っています！
English: What are you saying? Blood traces were undeniably found on that locker!

Japanese: いやいやいや、だから！ そんなコト、ぼくに聞かれても
English: Don't look at me, I didn't put it there!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！ カガク的に考えてみましょう！
English: Mr. Wright! Think it through scientifically!

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃん‥‥！
English: Ema!

Japanese: あの日の午後‥‥‥
English: On that afternoon...

Japanese: タイホくんを保管室に運んだのは、 原灰巡査ですよね。
English: Officer Meekins was the one who brought the Blue Badger to the evidence room, right?

Japanese: それ以降、後ろの保管庫に “手のアト”を残すのは、不可能。
English: After he put it down, it would be impossible to leave a handprint on that locker.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ということは、この“血痕”は‥‥
English: So that must mean this blood mark...

Japanese: タイホくんが置かれるより “以前”につけられた‥‥？
English: was left there before the Blue Badger was brought in...?

Japanese: 待ってください！ いくらなんでも、それは‥‥！
English: Just one moment! I will not allow such farfetched balderdash in my courtroom!

Japanese: たしかに、信じがたい‥‥でも。 そう考えるしかありません！
English: It may sound farfetched, Your Honor...
but it's the only possible explanation!

Japanese: ‥‥２月２１日。 あの日、証拠保管室では‥‥
English: On February 21st... in the Police Department's evidence room...

Japanese: “２回”、血が 流されたコトになります！
English: blood was spilled not once, but twice!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: B-but how...!

Japanese: １回は、この防犯カメラに 映っている“事件”‥‥
English: One time was captured on this tape, taken by the security camera.

Japanese: 原灰巡査が手をケガした、 ササヤカなものでした。
English: Officer Meekins cut his hand, from which a trivial amount of blood fell.

Japanese: そして‥‥モンダイは、 “もう１つ”の事件です。
English: The problem is... the "other" time.

Japanese: この格闘より“以前”にも、 保管室では、血が流された。
English: Someone bled prior to the struggle shown on this tape.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: It had to have been...

Japanese: それこそが、“ホンモノ”の 多田敷捜査官の殺害事件なのです！
English: Detective Goodman, when he was really murdered!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなコトを！ その可能性は、否定された！
English: That's ridiculous! I refute you!

Japanese: たしかに、防犯カメラの映像は “ニセモノ”でした！
English: The murder portrayed in the security tape has been proven to be a fake.

Japanese: しかし！　それだけでは、 保管庫の血痕に、説明がつかない！
English: However!
That does not explain the blood mark found on the locker!

Japanese: それでは‥‥キサマの言う “もう１つ”の事件は‥‥
English: So then... assuming this "murder" you purport really happened.

Japanese: いったい“いつ” 起こったというのだ！
English: When did it take place!?

Japanese: 事件が起こったことを示す、 証拠の提示を要求するッ！
English: I demand you show evidence that proves it occurred!

Japanese: （“１回目”の事件が 起こった時間‥‥）
English: (When did the first incident occur...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の主張は こういうことですね？
English: To surmise, the defense claims that...

Japanese: 多田敷さんに変装した罪門　恭介が 原灰巡査をキズつけるより、以前。
English: prior to Officer Meekins being cut by Jake Marshall, who was disguised as Detective

Japanese: あの日の証拠保管室では、 “もう１つの事件”があった。
English: Goodman, another "incident" took place in that evidence room.

Japanese: 保管庫の血痕が、 それを証明しています。
English: The blood mark on the locker proves this.

Japanese: では‥‥いったい、その “１回目”の事件は‥‥
English: Very well. Then tell us...

Japanese: “いつ”起こったのでしょうか？ その手がかりが必要です。
English: When did this "first" incident occur? Proof must be presented.

Japanese: （犯行時刻を示す手がかり‥‥ １つだけ、ある！）
English: (Proof that shows when the murder took place...)
(There's only one piece of evidence that can show that!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護側に 提示していただきましょう！
English: Now then. Will the defense please present its evidence?

Japanese: “１回目”の犯行が行われた 時刻を示す、証拠品とは！
English: What shows when the "first" crime took place?

Japanese: これが、その証拠です！ ‥‥カンタンなコトですよ。
English: This is the proof! It's quite simple, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコトなら まちがわないでもらいたいな。
English: If it's so simple, then don't get it wrong.

Japanese: あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: “あれ”で済めば、 ケーサツはいりません！
English: If "huh" was an acceptable excuse, we wouldn't need police.

Japanese: “１回目”の犯行は、タイホくんが 置かれる前に起こったんですよね。
English: The first crime took place before the Blue Badger was brought to the room, right?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥タイホくんは、“いつ” 置かれたんでしたっけ。
English: Say... When was the Blue Badger brought there, anyway?

Japanese: たしか‥‥“時刻”がわかる 証拠品が、どこかにあったね。
English: Don't we have a time chart among our evidence items that lists the time?

Japanese: 事件が証拠保管室で起きた以上、 この部屋に入らなければならない。
English: If the crime took place inside the evidence room, then the perpetrator would had to have

Japanese: そして‥‥そのためには、 ＩＤカードが必要です。
English: entered the room. In order to do so, an ID card is required.

Japanese: ＩＤカード‥‥‥あッ！ 《使用記録》ですかッ！
English: An ID card... Oh! The ID Card Record!

Japanese: 原灰巡査が、タイホくんのパネルを 持ちこんだ時刻は‥‥
English: Officer Meekins brought the Blue Badger panel into the evidence room at...

Japanese: 《４時５０分》でしょうな、 モチロン。
English: Let's see here... 4:50 PM.

Japanese: １回目の事件は、“それ以前”に 起こったわけですから‥‥
English: If the crime took place before that time, then it would be...

Japanese: ああああああああああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAHHH!

Japanese: み。み。み。み。み。み。み。み。
English: M-M-M-M-M-M-M-M-

Japanese: みみみみみみみみみみ、御剣検事！ あなた、なんというコトをッ！
English: M-M-M-M-M-M-M- Miles Edgeworth! Just what have you done!?

Japanese: まさか‥‥アンタだったとはなァ、 御剣のボウヤ。
English: I never figured you had nerve, boy.

Japanese: しらじらしいな‥‥証人。
English: Put off the act, witness.

Japanese: わかっているはずだ。 ‥‥考えるまでもあるまい。
English: It doesn't take a lot of thought to figure out it couldn't have been me.

Japanese: フン‥‥‥！ ザンネンながら、ね。
English: Hmm... Nope, I ain't gettin' it...

Japanese: フン‥‥‥！ 考えても、わかりません。
English: Hmm... I'm afraid I don't understand either.

Japanese: 現場のルミノール反応を見れば、 そこで“流血”があったのは明白。
English: It's clear from the luminol test that blood was there.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。“２回目”の 事件が起こった際‥‥
English: However, when the "second" crime took place...

Japanese: 原灰巡査も、罪門　恭介も、 その痕跡に気づいていない。
English: both Officer Meekins and Officer Marshall failed to notice the blood.

Japanese: つまり‥‥“１回目”の事件の 痕跡は、インメツされたわけだ‥‥
English: That means... the blood from the first crime was wiped away...

Japanese: “真犯人”によって。
English: by the real murderer.

Japanese: 被害者を殺害して、死体を運び、 床に残った血痕を処理する‥‥
English: I would have had just ten minutes to murder the victim, carry his body away,

Japanese: 私に与えられた時間は、１０分。 ‥‥できるはずがない。
English: and clean up the blood... Unfortunately that's physically impossible.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥
English: That would mean...

Japanese: 犯行は、さらに《前》に 起こったことになります！
English: the crime must have taken place before Mr. Edgeworth entered the evidence room.

Japanese: もう一度、記録を見てみましょう。
English: Let's look at the chart again.
There's only one other card number remaining: "7777777"!

Japanese: とんだラッキーナンバーだね、 コイツは‥‥
English: Talk about a lucky number...

Japanese: し‥‥しかし。 それでは、不自然です！
English: But wait... That doesn't make sense!

Japanese: 被害者自身‥‥多田敷捜査官が、 保管室に入れないではないですか！
English: How could Detective Goodman have entered the evidence room?

Japanese: 使用記録がない以上‥‥ 《真犯人といっしょに入った》
English: Since there's no record of his card being used beforehand...
he must have entered along with the real murderer.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるしかありません。 《７７７７７７７》の人物と！
English: That's the only plausible explanation.
He went in with "7777777"!

Japanese: 御剣検事！ 大至急、調べてください！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Please look into this ASAP!

Japanese: なななななななななななななななの ＩＤの人物を！
English: Find out whose ID number is "sevensevensevensevenseven- sevensevenseven"!

Japanese: “な”が１回多いようだ、裁判長。
English: That's one "seven" too many, Your Honor.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か‥‥そのナンバーを 調べることは不可能だ。
English: Unfortunately... I'm unable to look up the owner of that ID card.

Japanese: 少なくとも‥‥今の時点では。
English: At least, at present.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: What!?

Japanese: どういうコトだ？　ボウヤ‥‥
English: Explain yourself, son.

Japanese: 《７７７７７７７》 このナンバーは、警察署長以上‥‥
English: The ID number "7777777" belongs to someone with a rank of Captain or higher...

Japanese: いわゆる“上層部”と呼ばれる グループのものだ。
English: Someone who is a so-called "executive officer."

Japanese: それを調べる権限は、キホン的に ‥‥我々には、ない。
English: We don't have the authority to inquire into such a person's identity.

Japanese: そんなバカな！ じゃあ、いったい‥‥
English: But that's ridiculous! Just how...

Japanese: 最後まで聞きたまえ。
English: I'm not finished talking, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 我々に、捜査権が発生する ケースが、１つだけある。
English: There is one situation in which we can be granted such authority.

Japanese: 上層部の特定の人物に、公式な 容疑が認められた場合、ですな。
English: If an official charge filed against an executive is accepted.

Japanese: ‥‥“公式な容疑”‥‥
English: An "official charge"...

Japanese: アンタたちは、いつもそうさ。 ‥‥身内をかばい合う。
English: You're all alike, aren't you?

Japanese: “いんぺい”と“ねつ造”‥‥ それが、検事局のやり方なのさ！
English: With your "cover-ups" and your "forgeries"...
That's how the Prosecutor's Office operates!

Japanese: ‥‥私は、自らのシゴトに ホコリを持っている。
English: I take pride in my work, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: コンキョのない中傷は やめてもらいたいな‥‥証人。
English: I would appreciate it if you would keep your slander to yourself.

Japanese: “中傷”‥‥？　おもしろい。
English: "Slander," is it? Okay.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥１つだけ。 質問させてもらうぜ。
English: Let me ask a question.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: Yes?

Japanese: アンタじゃない。 被告席に座ってる、カノジョだよ。
English: No, not to you. To her, the defendant sitting over there.

Japanese: アンタの言う“上層部”の代表さ。
English: Your own little "executive."

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥？）
English: (L-Lana?)

Japanese: ‥‥バカなコトを。 彼女には、容疑がかかっている。
English: Don't be stupid. She's been charged with murder.

Japanese: 当然、ＩＤナンバーは調べた。 ‥‥《７７７７７７７》ではない。 "7777777."
English: Of course we've looked up her ID number... and it's not

Japanese: そうじゃない。 わかってるだろう？　ボウヤ。
English: Don't play me for a fool, pardner. That's not what I want to ask.

Japanese: オレの知りたいのは、ただ１つ。 あの事件のコトだけさ。
English: All I want to know is one thing... about that incident.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ですか‥‥
English: The SL-9 Incident?

Japanese: 主席検事、答えてくれ！ ２年前の、あの裁判で‥‥
English: Answer me this, Chief Prosecutor!

Japanese: アンタたちは、本当に‥‥ 正当な証拠だけを使ったのか？
English: In that trial two years ago... Did you really only use legitimate evidence!?

Japanese: 質問は聞かれたと思うが‥‥ 主席検事。
English: Do you need the witness to repeat his question, Chief Prosecutor?

Japanese: うかがいました。
English: I heard him fine, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥私自身が この事件の審理を担当した。
English: Two years ago... I was in charge of the prosecution for that trial.

Japanese: 当然、我々は、違法な証拠など
English: At the time, we...

Japanese: ‥‥ときとして。
English: Occasionally... we felt the powerlessness of the law.

Japanese: 法の無力を感じることがあった。 ‥‥少なくとも、私は。
English: At least... I did.

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃん‥‥？
English: L-Lana...

Japanese: 私は、犯罪を法でしばるため、 このシゴトを選びました。
English: I became a prosecutor in order to suppress crime with the law.

Japanese: しかし、気がついてみれば。 しばられているのは、我々のほう。
English: But before I realized it, we were the ones being suppressed by the law.

Japanese: ひ、被告人！　あなたは‥‥ 何を言おうとしているのですか！
English: Defendant! Just what are you saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、聞こう。 主席検事サマ。
English: I'll ask you again, Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの裁判‥‥
English: During that trial two years ago...

Japanese: アンタたちは本当に、すべての 証拠を法廷に提示したかい？
English: did you really present all the evidence in court?

Japanese: ジッサイに捜査をしていた オレの目を見て、断言できるかい？
English: Can you look me, an investigator in that crime, in the eye and say that you did?

Japanese: 主席検事！　まさか、あなたは‥‥
English: Chief Prosecutor! You didn't...

Japanese: その必要は認めません。
English: I don't have to, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ なぜだ！　なぜ、答えない‥‥
English: Why don't you answer him!?

Japanese: 真に恐ろしい犯罪には、 こちらも恐ろしい手を使う‥‥
English: Drastic crimes require drastic measures...

Japanese: いたしかたのないことです。
English: That's just the way it is.

Japanese: 彼に正当な裁きを与えるため‥‥ 私は、手段を選ばなかった。
English: We did what we had to... in order for him to get the verdict he deserved.

Japanese: そんな！　お、お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: But Lana!

Japanese: それが、たとえ‥‥‥“ねつ造”と 呼ばれる結果になろうとも。
English: Even if it involved "forging" evidence.

Japanese: つまり、そういうことだよ。 ‥‥御剣のボウヤ。
English: See? That's what I'm talking about.

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥‥ なんだとオオオオオオオオオオッ！
English: No... NNNNOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 静粛にィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! ORDERRRRR!!!

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんの発言は、 あまりに衝撃的だった‥‥
English: Lana's remarks caused such a stir...

Japanese: ‥‥騒ぎはおさまらず、 審理は中断。すべては明日‥‥
English: the chaos in the courtroom could not be quelled. The conclusion of the trial...

Japanese: ‥‥最終日に持ち越された‥‥
English: would have to wait until the following day...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
